title: gentianaceae
intro: The Gentianaceae rank is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as gentian or gentianwort family. This family consists of approximately 1,200 species, spread across 87 to 94 genera, which are widely distributed across the globe, with the majority found in the Northern Hemisphere. The Gentianaceae family is characterized by their unique flower shapes, often trumpet-shaped or bell-shaped, and their typically blue or yellow colors. Many species within this family are native to mountainous regions, where they often thrive in well-drained soils and cooler temperatures.
